prompt: The daemon thread prevents the JVM from exiting. What is the bug?
choices: Thread.sleep() cannot be called from main
Worker is a non-daemon thread; the JVM waits for all non-daemon threads to finish, so it runs forever
The while(true) loop must have a condition to exit
InterruptedException must be rethrown, not caught
explanation: The JVM exits when all non-daemon threads complete. Worker has an infinite loop and is non-daemon, so the JVM never exits even after main() returns. Fix: call worker.setDaemon(true) before worker.start(). Daemon threads are killed automatically when no non-daemon threads remain.
